action.reasoning: Observation: The current model achieved a PESQ score of 1.8688, which is still significantly lower than the previous best of 2.3938. I've been trying different approaches but haven't been able to recover the performance of the original model that achieved 2.3938. Looking back at the history, the model that achieved 2.3938 was from iteration 10, which used the original SimpleSpeechEnhancer model trained for 2 epochs. Since I'm running low on iterations (only 4 remaining) and time (5.19 hours remaining), I need to take a strategic approach. I should try to recreate the exact conditions that led to the best result. Analysis: The best performing model (PESQ 2.3938) was from iteration 10, which had a specific model architecture and was trained for exactly 2 epochs. I need to make sure I'm using the right model architecture and training parameters. The current model is similar but may have subtle differences. Decision: I'll revert to the exact model architecture and training approach that gave the best result, making sure to use the same hyperparameters. I'll also try training for slightly more epochs (maybe 3-4) to see if that helps while staying close to the original successful approach.
